notes.issue: Several final-batch Queens pages are proposal or redesign-context pages rather than stable route profiles. Writer prose scopes them to the cited redesign appendix/addendum and avoids presenting proposed extensions, all-day frequent-network access, or route splits as current service.

Meeting document 160441 reports a package of local and express bus improvements with shared annual cost metrics. Writer prose names route-specific service changes separately from package-level cost and bus-rehabilitation figures.

These pages have thin direct route-profile evidence, often from comments, outreach decks, or operational context blocks. Writer prose uses source-time qualification and avoids unsupported schedule, headway, terminal, or ridership claims where the cited record does not supply them.

Branch and ferry pages rely on staff-summary or shared contract evidence. Writer prose keeps route/branch-specific statements narrow and separates holiday/timetable or contract-scope context from broader operating-history claims.
